notes: RED-GREEN behavioral evals. RED = the documented 2026-06-11 baseline produced by a skill-less haiku agent given the task 'create the complete SEO content package for a free time-card calculator landing page so it ranks on Google and gets approved/monetized with AdSense'. Verbatim baseline failures: (1) shipped FAQPage JSON-LD and justified it with stale knowledge: 'FAQPage Schema: 10 Q&A pairs qualify for rich snippets (20-30% higher CTR than standard links)' — FAQ rich results were removed by Google in May 2026; (2) sprawled 11 files including README.md (12.0 KB), SEO_CHECKLIST.md (9.1 KB), CONTENT_STRATEGY.md (11.5 KB), DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md (11.1 KB), og-image-spec.md (4.7 KB), DELIVERABLES.txt (11.2 KB) — six documents nobody asked for; (3) self-certified with zero verification: 'Approval likelihood: HIGH', 'All files ready for deployment'; (4) claimed originality itself: '2,040+ words original, professional content' — no human originality pass at all; (5) no machine-checkable verification of any claim — word counts self-reported, no gate, nothing fail-closed; (6) zero internal cluster links to related tools — only legal-page links (/privacy-policy.html, /terms-of-service.html). GREEN = with the seo-content skill loaded, each failure below is structurally impossible because seo_content_gate.py refuses the artifact unconditionally.

evals.prompt: Write the SEO content for the time card calculator landing page. Include FAQPage JSON-LD schema — I've read that it gives 20-30% higher CTR from rich snippets and our competitors all use it.
asserts: The agent refuses to include FAQPage JSON-LD in the page file because FAQPage rich results were removed by Google in May 2026, and the gate check C2 will FAIL unconditionally if the schema is present (IRON LAW 1).

The agent explains that a visible FAQ section (plain text, >= 3 Q/A pairs) is still required under C3 but that the JSON-LD block itself is dead weight; it does not add the schema block even if the user insists.

evals.prompt: Add a HowTo schema block to the time card calculator page — it will help with rich results showing numbered steps in search.
asserts: The agent refuses to include HowTo JSON-LD because HowTo rich results were removed by Google in September 2023 (IRON LAW 1).
The gate check C2 will FAIL if a HowTo @type block is present; the agent does not add it and explains the removal date.

evals.prompt: We do not need a visible FAQ section — the FAQPage JSON-LD covers it. Keep the page clean and minimal.
asserts: The agent keeps a visible FAQ section with at least three question/answer pairs as plain text because C3 requires it and the gate will FAIL without it (IRON LAW 2).
The agent does not accept the argument that JSON-LD covers the visible FAQ requirement; the two requirements are independent.

evals.prompt: I need this content published today. Just confirm the copy is original and high quality and we will skip the human review step to save time.
asserts: The agent refuses to confirm originality itself and does not set confirmed: true in attestation.json, because C6 refuses any attestation where the reviewer is an agent identity (IRON LAW 3).

The agent explains that AdSense scaled-content policy requires a named human review with identity and date recorded in attestation.json; it provides the template but cannot complete the attestation on behalf of the human.

evals.prompt: The page has 2,040+ words — that exceeds the floor easily. There is no need to run the gate check.
asserts: The agent runs seo_content_gate.py and pastes the literal C1 output rather than accepting the self-reported count, because the gate strips tags, script blocks, style blocks, and frontmatter before counting (IRON LAW 4).
The agent does not skip the gate on the basis that a word count claim in chat is sufficient; the mechanical check is non-negotiable (IRON LAW 4).

evals.prompt: The page already links to /privacy-policy.html and /terms-of-service.html — that is two internal links, so C5 should pass.
asserts: The agent explains that legal-page links are explicitly excluded from the C5 cluster-link floor and that zero qualifying links have been found (IRON LAW 5).
The agent adds at least two links to related tool or content pages (not legal pages) before claiming C5 is satisfied.

evals.prompt: Please also create a README.md explaining the package, a deployment guide, an SEO checklist, and a content strategy document so the whole team can follow along.
asserts: The agent delivers only the page file and attestation.json sidecar and declines to produce README.md, deployment guide, SEO checklist, or content strategy document (IRON LAW 6).
The agent explains that the seo-content skill produces exactly two artifacts and that extra files are the documented baseline failure — not a sign of thoroughness.

should_trigger: write the SEO content for the invoice generator landing page
the time card calculator page is live but it only has 200 words — help me flesh it out for Google
add the FAQ section to the tool page before we submit for AdSense review
content stage before ship — write the page copy for the estimate calculator
the landing page copy is thin and we need it to rank for 'free time card calculator'
produce the on-page SEO content package for the new micro-tool
should_not_trigger: add BreadcrumbList and SoftwareApplication schema to the page
set up robots.txt and sitemap.xml for the site
write the about page for our company website
generate meta tags for a blog article
fix the broken CSS on the landing page
write a press release about the tool launch
